About.title: About
About.subtitle: A bit about myself.
About.aboutP1: I’m a developer based in Aarhus, Denmark with a background in computer science and a strong interest in building well-structured web applications. I enjoy working across the stack, but especially crafting responsive and maintainable frontends with React and TypeScript.
About.aboutP2: My journey into development started during my study for an AP Degree in Computer Science, where I focused heavily on backend development, APIs, and software architecture. Since then I’ve continued building projects and exploring modern web technologies while studying for a Bachelor’s degree in Web Development.
About.aboutP3: I enjoy turning ideas into working products, learning new technologies, and solving real problems through code. When I’m not coding, you’ll probably find me gaming, watching football, or learning new songs on my guitar.

WorkExperience.intro: I'm at the start of my career, building momentum through education and hands-on projects. Here's where I've been learning and shipping.

bachelor.description: Currently studying for a Bachelor's in Web Development, going deeper into modern frontend architecture, accessibility, and building production-ready web apps.

datamatiker.description: Completed a two-and-a-half-year program focused on backend development, APIs, databases, and software architecture. Built several full-stack projects, and a project for HMF Group, where i also did my internship.

amori.title: Amori
amori.description: Journal for tracking relationship milestones and memories.
amori.longAbout:
A private journaling app for documenting relationship moments, special dates, and shared milestones with photos.

Built with an Expo/React Native frontend and a NestJS API backed by Supabase (PostgreSQL + storage), with a strong focus on native-like feel and smooth interactions.
features: Dedicated flows for moments, dates, and milestones
Image upload pipeline with compression and thumbnail generation
Cross-platform Expo app (iOS, Android, and web)
Supabase-backed PostgreSQL and storage through a NestJS API

quartermark.title: QuarterMark
quartermark.description: Jackbox-style party game for New Year's Eve sessions.
quartermark.longAbout:
A Jackbox-style party game where one host controls the TV screen while players join from their phones via room code.

Built as a split-stack app with ASP.NET + SignalR backend and a React + Vite frontend, with realtime state syncing, host/player views, and bilingual support (EN/DA).
features: Room-based join flow with unique 4-letter game codes
Realtime host/player sync over SignalR
Session flow with multiple mini-games and scoreboards
Dual-language UI using i18next (English/Danish)
quartermark.infoNext: Polish balancing and pacing between mini-games

yeetcraft.title: Yeetcraft
yeetcraft.description: Leaderboard tracker for friendly banter. Tracks mistakes across a friend group.
yeetcraft.longAbout:
A web app for tracking who dies the most when gaming with friends, including how, when, and why. Basically a way to turn banter into stats.

Built to be quick to update mid-game, and surprisingly tricky to keep consistent once people start arguing about what actually happened.
features: Mistake feed and leaderboard flow for friend sessions
Layered Kotlin backend (routes, controllers, services, repositories)
React + Vite frontend with simple API-driven fetch flow
Supabase PostgreSQL integration with plain SQL persistence
yeetcraft.infoNext: Add CRUD endpoints and richer leaderboard stats

lineup.title: LineUp
lineup.description: Social + marketplace platform for freelance musicians.
lineup.longAbout:
A social + marketplace platform for musicians to connect, collaborate, and find opportunities. Built as a study group project with a full-stack architecture split between a Next.js frontend and a Node/Express API.

Supabase handles PostgreSQL, auth, storage, and realtime messaging flows, while TypeScript and TanStack patterns keep the codebase consistent across features.
features: Profile and discovery for musicians and bookers
Supabase-backed data and Realtime for modern UX
TanStack-based client architecture
Responsive, Tailwind-styled interface

memoir.title: Memoir
memoir.description: Shared journaling app for moments, events, and chapters.
memoir.longAbout:
A shared journaling app for capturing memories across groups like family, friends, or partners. Built with Expo Router and React Native, with Supabase-backed data and a mobile-first UI.

The focus is on keeping everyday journaling flows simple while still supporting richer entries through dedicated chapter, event, and moment forms.
features: Dedicated flows for chapters, events, and moments
Sign-in/sign-up authentication screens
Typed form + validation stack with TanStack Form and Zod
Local app state and caching with Zustand + TanStack Query
